notes: Platform-specific adaptations. All copy is measured, authoritative, source-first. Never sensational.

week1.theme: Chapter Spotlight: Jekyll Island & the Federal Reserve

content:
In November 1910, six men representing 1/4 of the world's wealth boarded a private rail car in New Jersey.

Their destination: Jekyll Island, Georgia.
Their mission: draft the blueprint for a new central bank.

Every detail is sourced. Here's the documented record. 🧵

Senator Nelson Aldrich organized the trip under a false cover story — they claimed to be duck hunting.

Attendees included representatives from J.P. Morgan, Rockefeller, and the Rothschild banking interests.

Source: The Aldrich Papers, Library of Congress.

The bill they drafted became the Federal Reserve Act of 1913.

It was passed two days before Christmas, when most Congress members had gone home.

Source: Congressional Record, 63rd Congress, December 23, 1913.

The Federal Reserve is not federal. It has no reserves. It is a private banking cartel with a government charter.

Read the full documented chapter — with every claim sourced to primary documents:
https://veritasworldwide.com/chapter/chapter-3

Every claim in The Record is classified:

✓ Verified — court records, congressional testimony
◐ Circumstantial — documented but interpretive
⚠ Disputed — sworn testimony, not confirmed

Judge the evidence yourself.
https://veritasworldwide.com/methodology

posts.content:
The Jekyll Island meeting of 1910 is one of the most consequential secret gatherings in American financial history — and one of the most thoroughly documented.

Six men, representing approximately one-quarter of the world's wealth, met on a private island to draft what would become the Federal Reserve Act.

At Veritas Worldwide Press, we've compiled the primary source documentation into a single, free, open-access chapter. Every claim is classified by evidence tier and sourced to congressional records, personal papers, and financial archives.

Read the full chapter: https://veritasworldwide.com/chapter/chapter-3

Use existing content pack: Jekyll Island carousel (10 slides). Caption: 'In 1910, six men secretly drafted the blueprint for the Federal Reserve on a private island. Every fact sourced to primary documents. Link in bio for the full chapter. #PrimarySources #DocumentaryHistory #FederalReserve'

Discussion question: The Federal Reserve Act was passed two days before Christmas 1913, when most Congress members had gone home. The meeting that drafted it took place in secret.

Should the creation of a nation's central bank require this level of secrecy? What does the historical record tell us?

Read the sourced chapter: https://veritasworldwide.com/chapter/chapter-3

In 1967, the CIA issued Dispatch 1035-960: a classified memo instructing field assets on how to counter criticism of the Warren Commission Report.

It was declassified in 1976.

This is not theory. This is a government document.
https://veritasworldwide.com/chapter/foreword

Did you know the term 'conspiracy theory' was weaponized by a specific CIA memo?

Dispatch 1035-960, issued April 1, 1967. Declassified 1976. It instructed media contacts on how to discredit critics of the Warren Commission.

The document is real. Read it sourced: veritasworldwide.com/chapter/foreword

Title: CIA Dispatch 1035-960 (1967, declassified 1976) — the documented origin of 'conspiracy theory' as a dismissal tactic

Body: I've been compiling a documentary history sourced entirely to primary documents — court records, congressional testimony, declassified files. The foreword covers this CIA dispatch, which instructed field assets on countering criticism of the Warren Commission.

The full sourced text with evidence tier classification: https://veritasworldwide.com/chapter/foreword

Happy to answer questions about the sourcing methodology.

week1.theme: On This Day — Historical Tie-In (rotate weekly)

posts.content:
On this day in history: [Research specific date events from chapters]

Format: Date + documented event + primary source citation + chapter link.

Template: 'On [date], [event]. Source: [primary document]. Read the full documented record: [chapter URL]'
Story slide: 'ON THIS DAY' header + key fact + source citation + swipe-up to chapter. Use brand template with parchment bg + crimson accents.

How do you know what's verified vs. what's alleged?

Every claim in The Record is classified:

✓ VERIFIED — Court filings, congressional records, declassified documents
◐ CIRCUMSTANTIAL — Documented facts, interpretive connection
⚠ DISPUTED — Sworn testimony, not independently confirmed

Transparency > trust.
https://veritasworldwide.com/methodology

In an era of misinformation, the methodology matters more than the conclusion.

At Veritas Worldwide Press, every claim is classified into one of three evidence tiers — Verified, Circumstantial, or Disputed — with full source citations.

Readers can evaluate each piece of evidence independently. That's the standard investigative journalism should aspire to.

https://veritasworldwide.com/methodology

Hook: 'How do you know if something is actually true?'

Body: Show the three evidence tiers with visual examples. Green = court records. Amber = documented but interpretive. Red = alleged but unconfirmed.

CTA: 'Every chapter at veritasworldwide.com uses this system. Judge the evidence yourself.'

Duration: 30-45 seconds.

"Give me control of a nation's money supply, and I care not who makes its laws."
— Attributed to Mayer Amschel Rothschild (disputed — no primary source confirms this exact quote)

Evidence tier: ⚠ Disputed

We label what's verified and what isn't. That's the difference.
https://veritasworldwide.com/chapter/chapter-1
Pull quote graphic (1080x1080): Parchment background, crimson accent bar, Playfair Display quote, evidence tier badge, source citation, chapter link. Use brand kit assets.

Question for the community: If you could read the primary source documents behind any historical event covered in The Record, which chapter would you start with?

Weekend reading recommendation: If you're new to The Record, start with the Foreword — it explains our methodology and evidence standards before you encounter a single claim.

Then Chapter 9 (JFK) or Chapter 3 (Jekyll Island) are reader favorites.

All free: https://veritasworldwide.com
